{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Director, Customer Development & Digital Commerce, NEF",
  "description": "The Coca-Cola Company (NYSE: KO) is the world&#39;s largest beverage company, refreshing consumers with more than 500 sparkling and still brands. At The Coca-Cola Company you can cultivate your career in a challenging and dynamic environment. We are the largest manufacturer and distributor of nonalcoholic drinks in the world-selling more than 1 billion drinks a day. Unlock your full potential with a future-focused company that is known and respected throughout the world.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Director, Customer Development &amp; Digital Commerce, NEF
Job ID: R-135084
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reporting Line: Franchise

Role Purpose&nbsp;


	The Senior Director, Customer Development &amp; Digital Commerce leads the integration of Customer Development, Digital Commerce, and System-wide execution to drive sustainable volume, value, and market share growth.
	The role shapes and executes a unified Franchise-level customer and digital commerce strategy, accelerating omnichannel growth, strengthening execution excellence, and building future-ready commercial capabilities across the System.
	The role&nbsp;operates&nbsp;in a complex multi-bottler environment, orchestrating alignment across Franchise leadership, customer teams, bottling partners, and digital ecosystems to deliver enterprise results.&nbsp;


Key Accountabilities&nbsp;


	Define and execute a Franchise-wide Customer Development strategy across Modern Trade, E-Commerce, Q-Commerce, QSR,&nbsp;On-Premise, O2O, and Travel channels.&nbsp;
	Deliver profitable volume, value, and share growth aligned to Annual Business Plans and Long-Range Plans.&nbsp;
	&nbsp;Lead an integrated Digital Commerce and Digital Business Strategy embedded in commercial planning.&nbsp;
	Accelerate B2B, E-Commerce, and digital execution models across customers and bottlers.&nbsp;
	Navigate and align a 30+ bottler ecosystem, ensuring execution excellence and system collaboration.&nbsp;
	Develop strategic, value-creating customer and enterprise digital partnerships.&nbsp;
	Lead, coach, and develop a high-performing senior team and act as&nbsp;a capability&nbsp;multiplier across Franchise teams.&nbsp;


Experience &amp; Qualifications&nbsp;


	15+ years of progressive leadership experience within FMCG or consumer goods environments.&nbsp;
	Deep&nbsp;expertise&nbsp;in Customer Development, Commercial Strategy, Digital Commerce, or Operations.&nbsp;
	Proven success delivering results in complex, multi-market&nbsp;and multi-partner systems.&nbsp;
	Strong&nbsp;people&nbsp;leadership, executive influence, and change leadership capability.&nbsp;


Critical Capabilities:


	General management capability with P&amp;L accountability.&nbsp;
	Strategic and systems thinking with strong execution orientation.&nbsp;
	Agile and change leadership mindset.&nbsp;
	Deep customer, commercial, and digital ecosystem understanding.&nbsp;
	Data-driven storytelling and executive communication skills.&nbsp;
	Business Development, Business Planning, Collaborative Leadership, Communication, Consultative Sales Management, Customer Relationship Management (CRM), Group Problem Solving, Influencing, Long Term Planning, Operational Assessment, Sales Forecasting, Sales Management, Sales Process, Solutions Selling, Waterfall Model
",

Senior Director, Customer Development & Digital Commerce, NEF Job at Coca-Cola Company




        
    

        
        The Coca-Cola Company (NYSE: KO) is the world's largest beverage company, refreshing consumers with more than 500 sparkling and still brands. At The Coca-Cola Company you can cultivate your career in a challenging and dynamic environment. We are the largest manufacturer and distributor of nonalcoholic drinks in the world-selling more than 1 billion drinks a day. Unlock your full potential with a future-focused company that is known and respected throughout the world.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Director, Customer Development & Digital Commerce, NEF
Job ID: R-135084
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reporting Line: Franchise
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Role Purpose 


	The Senior Director, Customer Development & Digital Commerce leads the integration of Customer Development, Digital Commerce, and System-wide execution to drive sustainable volume, value, and market share growth.
	The role shapes and executes a unified Franchise-level customer and digital commerce strategy, accelerating omnichannel growth, strengthening execution excellence, and building future-ready commercial capabilities across the System.
	The role operates in a complex multi-bottler environment, orchestrating alignment across Franchise leadership, customer teams, bottling partners, and digital ecosystems to deliver enterprise results. 


Key Accountabilities 


	Define and execute a Franchise-wide Customer Development strategy across Modern Trade, E-Commerce, Q-Commerce, QSR, On-Premise, O2O, and Travel channels. 
	Deliver profitable volume, value, and share growth aligned to Annual Business Plans and Long-Range Plans. 
	 Lead an integrated Digital Commerce and Digital Business Strategy embedded in commercial planning. 
	Accelerate B2B, E-Commerce, and digital execution models across customers and bottlers. 
	Navigate and align a 30+ bottler ecosystem, ensuring execution excellence and system collaboration. 
	Develop strategic, value-creating customer and enterprise digital partnerships. 
	Lead, coach, and develop a high-performing senior team and act as a capability multiplier across Franchise teams. 


Experience & Qualifications 


	15+ years of progressive leadership experience within FMCG or consumer goods environments. 
	Deep expertise in Customer Development, Commercial Strategy, Digital Commerce, or Operations. 
	Proven success delivering results in complex, multi-market and multi-partner systems. 
	Strong people leadership, executive influence, and change leadership capability. 


Critical Capabilities:


	General management capability with P&L accountability. 
	Strategic and systems thinking with strong execution orientation. 
	Agile and change leadership mindset. 
	Deep customer, commercial, and digital ecosystem understanding. 
	Data-driven storytelling and executive communication skills. 
	Business Development, Business Planning, Collaborative Leadership, Communication, Consultative Sales Management, Customer Relationship Management (CRM), Group Problem Solving, Influencing, Long Term Planning, Operational Assessment, Sales Forecasting, Sales Management, Sales Process, Solutions Selling, Waterfall Model
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Asset Document Controller",
  "description": "Vagan Oil and Gas Limited is a Nigerian Private Limited liability Company registered under the Companies Acts of 1990, In line with the policies of the Nigerian service provision. The company core area of operation is to provide a broad spectrum of services ranging from Engineering, Pipeline &amp; Flowline Construction / Repairs, Facility piping and maintenance, Wellhead &amp; Christmas tree maintenance and servicing, Composite wrap technology, Equipment &amp; Offshore/Marine Supplies, etc.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Asset Document Controller

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Develop &amp; maintain document mgt system/control processes for product documentation and ISO certified management system.
	Serve as the district document and data custodian(s) creating and maintaining livelink workspace and the EDMS.
	Maintain the production asset document control library (manual/electronic files) consisting of product related documentation, drawings, technical manuals, product/process software, external standards, validation reports, HSE and training records, internal and external audit reports and department procedures and work instructions.
	Ensure that documents are revised and processed in a timely manner, according to the change control process, managing variety &amp; version control.
	Provide inputs from plant operating experience necessary to effectively update current plant operating manuals as may be required.
	Effectively manage book loans and document delivery to ensure no losses.
	Interface effectively with project team on related documents/manual.
	Manage handover of technical manuals/documents from the project to the asset team.
	Track outstanding brown field scope project manuals.
	Carry out proper tagging of all documents, manual racks etc. and establish electronic links to manage them.
	Run the EPG pilot of the GLMS system and serve as super users.
	Manage the upgrade of existing library as a fall out of their reports on its inadequacy following numerous projects.
	Act as focal point for EPG File-Plan project.
	Collate and analyze production and maintenance performance data against PEB business plan district.
	Management of projects library, general administrative activities, mail collection, dispatch and delivery, document control, flight booking and in emergency serve as back up HLO/HLAs.
	Putting in place an efficient data management system to enhance a flawless handover and effective management of the system, post commissioning and project delivery.
	Receiving and coding of all project documentation under a structured Electronic Data
	Management System (EDMS) and Document Archiving /Retrieval System.


Qualification required


	B.Sc. / B.Eng. / HND + 10yrs plus relevant working experience + Data Management certification(s) in relevant areas.
	Ten (10) years of Minimum working experience in any offshore production facility FPSO.
	Proficient in the use of SAP-Material Management Module and Microsoft office.
	Effective communication in written and spoken English.


Experience:


	Not less than 10 years engineering experience
	Not less than 5 years&rsquo; document controller experience in the Oil and Gas or Engineering Industry
	Good Communication Skills
	Knowledge of reading drawings and interpreting drawings would be an added advantage
	Proven experience supporting brownfield and greenfield projects as well as operating assets.
	Strong understanding of oil &amp; gas engineering documentation and asset lifecycle requirements.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications and CV to:&nbsp;info@vagangroup.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Senior Asset Document Controller Job at Vagan Oil and Gas Limited




        
    

        
        Vagan Oil and Gas Limited is a Nigerian Private Limited liability Company registered under the Companies Acts of 1990, In line with the policies of the Nigerian service provision. The company core area of operation is to provide a broad spectrum of services ranging from Engineering, Pipeline & Flowline Construction / Repairs, Facility piping and maintenance, Wellhead & Christmas tree maintenance and servicing, Composite wrap technology, Equipment & Offshore/Marine Supplies, etc.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Asset Document Controller

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop & maintain document mgt system/control processes for product documentation and ISO certified management system.
	Serve as the district document and data custodian(s) creating and maintaining livelink workspace and the EDMS.
	Maintain the production asset document control library (manual/electronic files) consisting of product related documentation, drawings, technical manuals, product/process software, external standards, validation reports, HSE and training records, internal and external audit reports and department procedures and work instructions.
	Ensure that documents are revised and processed in a timely manner, according to the change control process, managing variety & version control.
	Provide inputs from plant operating experience necessary to effectively update current plant operating manuals as may be required.
	Effectively manage book loans and document delivery to ensure no losses.
	Interface effectively with project team on related documents/manual.
	Manage handover of technical manuals/documents from the project to the asset team.
	Track outstanding brown field scope project manuals.
	Carry out proper tagging of all documents, manual racks etc. and establish electronic links to manage them.
	Run the EPG pilot of the GLMS system and serve as super users.
	Manage the upgrade of existing library as a fall out of their reports on its inadequacy following numerous projects.
	Act as focal point for EPG File-Plan project.
	Collate and analyze production and maintenance performance data against PEB business plan district.
	Management of projects library, general administrative activities, mail collection, dispatch and delivery, document control, flight booking and in emergency serve as back up HLO/HLAs.
	Putting in place an efficient data management system to enhance a flawless handover and effective management of the system, post commissioning and project delivery.
	Receiving and coding of all project documentation under a structured Electronic Data
	Management System (EDMS) and Document Archiving /Retrieval System.


Qualification required


	B.Sc. / B.Eng. / HND + 10yrs plus relevant working experience + Data Management certification(s) in relevant areas.
	Ten (10) years of Minimum working experience in any offshore production facility FPSO.
	Proficient in the use of SAP-Material Management Module and Microsoft office.
	Effective communication in written and spoken English.


Experience:


	Not less than 10 years engineering experience
	Not less than 5 years’ document controller experience in the Oil and Gas or Engineering Industry
	Good Communication Skills
	Knowledge of reading drawings and interpreting drawings would be an added advantage
	Proven experience supporting brownfield and greenfield projects as well as operating assets.
	Strong understanding of oil & gas engineering documentation and asset lifecycle requirements.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications and CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
